name: libxml-xpath-perl
title: libxml-xpath-perl
description:
Perl module for processing XPath
The XML::XPath module implements the W3C's XPath specification.  It aims to
be completely correct to the letter of the spec, and yet still allow
extensions through the use of functions and variables, such as are
provided by XSLT and XPointer.
.
Besides the Perl modules it also provides a command line tool named
"xpath" to query XPath statements in XML documents.

commands.name: xpath
commands.description: a script to query XPath statements in XML documents.

arguments.description: Never use an external DTD, ie. instantiate the XML::Parser module with 'ParseParamEnt => 0'.
arguments.raw: -n  Never use an external DTD, ie. instantiate the XML::Parser module with 'ParseParamEnt => 0'.

arguments.description: Be quiet. Output only errors (and no separator) on stderr.
arguments.raw: -q  Be quiet. Output only errors (and no separator) on stderr.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use xpath because Perl module for processing XPath.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use xpath as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `xpath [-s suffix] [-p prefix] [-n] [-q] -e query [-e query] ... [file] ...` as documented upstream for xpath.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair xpath with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `xpath --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.description: Never use an external DTD, ie. instantiate the XML::Parser module with 'ParseParamEnt => 0'.

arguments.name: -q
arguments.description: Be quiet. Output only errors (and no separator) on stderr.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install libxml-xpath-perl. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `xpath [-s suffix] [-p prefix] [-n] [-q] -e query [-e query] ... [file] ...`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Perl module for processing XPath.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: xpath - a script to query XPath statements in XML documents.
